_comment: nfetch's stdout is a live network body, so it CANNOT golden-conform on the plain runtime yet. The golden lands once nano-conformance.mjs gains NET record/replay: it serves the `net[].response` fixture for `net[].url` over the /dev/__net__ bridge (work in progress in tools/nano-conformance.mjs). Until then stdoutSha256 stays TBD; capture.yml builds the ELF and soft-fails the capture (the bridge device is unsupported in the harness), leaving TBD untouched. The recorded HTTP/1.1 response is test/fixtures/example.com.http — once replayed, the body after the first CRLFCRLF is what nfetch prints, and the golden captures deterministically.
